resposta:
solution:
```
class Solution:
    def minChanges(self, nums: List[int], k: int) -> int:
        n = len(nums)
        prefix = [0] * (n + 1)
        for i in range(n):
            prefix[i + 1] = prefix[i] ^ nums[i]
        res = n
        for i in range(k - 1, n):
            target = prefix[i - k + 1]
            cnt = sum(prefix[j + k] ^ prefix[j] == target for j in range(i - k + 1, n - k + 1))
            res = min(res, n - cnt)
        return res
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses prefix XOR array to efficiently calculate the XOR of any segment. Then it iterates over the array to find the minimum number of changes needed to make the XOR of all segments of size k equal to zero.
